key_insight: A large autoregressive transformer pretrained on massive catalyst data can generate catalyst structures conditioned on both discrete labels and continuous properties, enabling controllable inverse design.
engineering_relevance: This approach can speed up reaction-targeted catalyst discovery by producing structurally valid, optimized candidates with improved property matching, reducing reliance on exhaustive screening.
method: Pretrained a GPT-style generative model on 133 million catalyst structures.
Fine-tuned on ~460,000 optimized structures with categorical properties and binding energies.
Used numerical embeddings to condition generation on both categorical and continuous targets in one autoregressive framework.
evidence_signal: The model achieved 98% structural validity, 95% optimization validity, 93% joint match for adsorbate type and composition, and about 20% match for binding-energy conditioning, with 1.5 to 4-fold screening-efficiency gains.
watch_item: Binding-energy control remains modest, so real-world deployment may require tighter validation across unseen chemistries and synthesis constraints.
paper_title: Toward Controllable Catalyst Inverse Design via Large-Scale Autoregressive Pretraining
